name: Sopa Azteca
recipeIngredient: 1 bell pepper, red, cored, seeded, and sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, heat chicken broth.
2. In a large skillet, saute onions and garlic in olive oil for 10 minutes.
3. Add tomatoes, oregano, basil, and pepper. Saute another 10 minutes, until tomatoes are softened. Blend all the ingredients together in a blender.
4. Add the blended mixture to the hot broth. Add celery, bay leaves, red bell pepper, zucchini, and carrot. Let simmer for about ten minutes.
5. Meanwhile, fry tortilla strips in oil until crispy. Drain on paper towels. In the same oil, fry potatoes for 5 minutes. Add them to the soup mixture with a slotted spoon.
6. Add spinach, washed, to the soup. Turn off the heat under the soup and season with salt if needed.
7. Place avocados in a serving bowl and add a few drops of lemon juice to prevent discoloration.
8. Serve the soup by placing 3-4 strips of tortilla at the bottom of each bowl. Pour hot soup over, making sure to get some of everything except bay leaves, which should be discarded. Top with avocado and cheese.
